text: When working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. The server often responds with JSON, a lightweight format that is easy to read and structure. Many APIs require authentication, usually via an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the service. It’s also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain period. Finally, you should always include error handling in your code. This helps manage problems like network failures or unexpected responses, making your application more reliable and user-friendly.
